messages.content:
A trade caravan travels for one season. Track its state through the
events below, in order.

1. At dawn the caravan carries 7 crates of mirebeans.
2. At dawn the caravan carries 6 crates of hollowfern.
3. At dawn the caravan carries 6 crates of duskwax.
4. At dawn the caravan carries 7 crates of tallowroot.
5. Crew at dawn: Ashka, Odo, Sessa.
6. At the salt road the caravan takes on 3 crates of pinnowseed.
7. At the salt road the caravan loses 5 crates of hollowfern.
8. Odo leaves the crew at Gullwatch.
9. Ashka leaves the crew at Weirmarket.
10. If more than 6 crates of mirebeans remain, 3 are sold; otherwise 1 more are bought.
11. Tolvek joins the crew at Cinderholt.
12. At Harrow Fen the caravan trades away 5 crates of hollowfern.
13. If more than 6 crates of mirebeans remain, 3 are sold; otherwise 4 more are bought.
14. All remaining mirebeans crates are repacked and counted as tallowroot from here on.
15. At Weirmarket the caravan loses 1 crates of hollowfern.
16. Brint joins the crew at the tollgate.
17. Ferrin joins the crew at Cinderholt.
18. If more than 8 crates of pinnowseed remain, 2 are sold; otherwise 1 more are bought.

How many crates of pinnowseed does the caravan carry at the end?

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

Bell-foundry commission ledger, in clerk order (not day order).
Line format: C<id> d<day>: <patron as written> b<bells> f<fee in marks>.
Amend d<n>: C<id> f=<marks> or b=<bells> corrects that field; the latest day wins.
Names not listed in a Note belong to different patrons.
Note: Ostren Lodge also signs as 'Ostren & Kin'.
Note: Torbek House also signs as 'Torbek & Kin' or 'Torbeks'.
Note: Sorvin Lodge also signs as 'Sorvin & Kin'.

After all amendments, how many bells does commission 17 call for?

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

Five rune wards seal a vault door: ando, drell, wex, osk, quen.
Each ward holds one whole-number charge from 1 to 9. Charges may
repeat unless a binding forbids it. The warding script declares:

1. The wex charge is exactly 2 more than the osk charge.
2. The drell charge is strictly greater than the wex charge.
3. The wex and osk charges are not equal.
4. The osk charge is exactly 3 more than the quen charge.
5. Together, ando and drell hold exactly 17.
6. The drell charge is exactly 1 more than the ando charge.
7. The ando and quen charges add up to exactly 10.
8. The drell ward holds more than the ando ward.
9. The drell and quen wards hold different charges.

Either the bindings force a single possible assignment of charges,
or they contradict one another and cannot all hold at once.
If they force one assignment, give the charge of the wex
ward. If they contradict, give exactly the word IMPOSSIBLE.

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

The Tarnmoot fair is granting market stalls. Each stall pays a fee
(in gilds) and needs footage (in spans). Grant a subset of stalls so
the total fee is as large as possible while every rule holds.

Stalls:
S1 Kettlebright (toys): fee 34, footage 6
S2 Snugharbor (cloth): fee 20, footage 5
S3 Oakhollow (spice): fee 31, footage 4
S4 Thistledown (toys): fee 44, footage 8
S5 Umbergate (cloth): fee 47, footage 9
S6 Mossgable (toys): fee 53, footage 4
S7 Plumwhistle (food): fee 34, footage 9
S8 Rushlight (cloth): fee 60, footage 9
S9 Copperfen (food): fee 28, footage 3
S10 Gorsequill (cloth): fee 46, footage 2
S11 Hobbleworth (toys): fee 58, footage 4
S12 Pinchwick (spice): fee 20, footage 5
S13 Juniperloft (food): fee 9, footage 9
S14 Inkmoss (spice): fee 22, footage 5

Rules:
- Total footage of granted stalls must not exceed 55 spans.
- S2 and S11 are rivals: do not grant both.
- S3 and S10 are rivals: do not grant both.
- S8 and S9 are rivals: do not grant both.
- Grant at most 3 cloth stalls.
- Grant at most 2 spice stalls.

Reply with the ids of the granted stalls, comma-separated, any order.

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

A trade caravan travels for one season. Track its state through the
events below, in order.

1. At dawn the caravan carries 4 crates of emberwool.
2. At dawn the caravan carries 9 crates of mirebeans.
3. At the salt road the caravan loses 6 crates of quillnut.
4. At Gullwatch the caravan buys 5 crates of emberwool.
5. At the ford the caravan trades away 4 crates of quillnut.
6. At Harrow Fen the caravan buys 3 crates of mirebeans.

How many crates of mirebeans does the caravan carry at the end?

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

Three rune wards seal a vault door: osk, thorn, ando.
Each ward holds one whole-number charge from 1 to 9. Charges may
repeat unless a binding forbids it. The warding script declares:

1. The thorn ward holds exactly 2.
2. The osk and ando charges add up to exactly 7.
3. The osk and thorn charges add up to exactly 8.
4. The thorn charge is exactly 1 more than the ando charge.

Either the bindings force a single possible assignment of charges,
or they contradict one another and cannot all hold at once.
If they force one assignment, give the charge of the osk
ward. If they contradict, give exactly the word IMPOSSIBLE.

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

The Tarnmoot fair is granting market stalls. Each stall pays a fee
(in gilds) and needs footage (in spans). Grant a subset of stalls so
the total fee is as large as possible while every rule holds.

Stalls:
S1 Quaverley (herb): fee 22, footage 8

S3 Gorsequill (food): fee 13, footage 9
S4 Pinchwick (herb): fee 37, footage 2
S5 Kettlebright (food): fee 10, footage 5
S6 Hobbleworth (herb): fee 40, footage 3
